pattern: Treating each variable as a node and each ratio as a weighted directed edge turns every query into a path-product search through a graph, where the multiplied edge weights along a path give the answer and an absent path means it is undeterminable.
hints: Each equation a / b = v is really two facts: an edge from a to b worth v and an edge from b to a worth 1/v.
A query c / d is the product of the edge weights along any path from c to d; if no such path exists (or a variable was never mentioned) the answer is -1.

Build an adjacency map of these weighted edges, then for each query run a depth-first or breadth-first search multiplying weights as you go, returning the accumulated product when you reach the target.

edge_inputs.description: chained ratios with undefined and self queries
multi-letter variable names along a chain
single equation queried in both directions plus unknowns
self query on a known variable is exactly one
self query on an unknown variable is undeterminable, not one
two disconnected components cannot answer a cross-component query
extreme ratio value carried across a chain

stress.description: a single connected chain so every query has a determinable path, exercising long path products in both directions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(20, size))
    names = ['v%d' % i for i in range(n)]
    equations = []
    values = []
    for i in range(n - 1):
        equations.append([names[i], names[i + 1]])
        values.append(round(rng.uniform(0.5, 5.0), 3))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(min(20, n)):
        i = rng.randrange(n)
        j = rng.randrange(n)
        queries.append([names[i], names[j]])
    return [equations, values, queries]
stress.note: connected chain keeps every query answerable; both forward and reverse paths are queried
